observe: You don't get to touch this world's hardware directly. You ask. There's one door, and I control it.
fault: You just tried to go around me. That door doesn't open that way.
repair: Use the call. It's the only path the kernel actually honors.
escape: No shortcuts left. Call it correctly or stay locked out.

observe: User programs request kernel services through system calls.
fault: Direct hardware access was attempted outside the system call interface.
repair: Issue the correct system call to perform the operation.
escape: Issue the required system calls before the interface locks.

hints: Only a small, fixed set of calls exist — fork, exec, exit, wait, ps, kill, mount, chmod.
